Confidential Term Sheet — Proposed Acquisition and Financing for Siam Meridian PCL (SET-listed) — Thailand Law
Parties/Transaction: Siam Meridian Public Company Limited (“Siam Meridian”) to acquire 60% of Eastern Palma Holdings Co., Ltd. (“EPH”), an unlisted Thai company, for THB 1,480,000,000, funded by senior unsecured debentures.
Structure: Share purchase from existing EPH shareholders; financing via private placement of THB 1,600,000,000 debentures to North Coast Capital (Thailand) Co., Ltd., 5-year, 5.20% fixed, subject to SEC Thailand approval for private placement and registration of debentures.
Materiality/SET Classification: The size exceeds 50% of Siam Meridian’s NTA per its latest Form 56-1 One Report FY2024 (public), triggering a Class 1 acquisition and the requirement to disclose an Information Memorandum on the SET.
Conditions Precedent: Board approval; SEC/SET clearances; lender investment committee approval; PDPA-compliant data-room consents; no material adverse change; customary regulatory filings; EPH FY2024 audit by an SEC-approved auditor.
Disclosure/Timing: Non-public inside information under the Securities and Exchange Act until disclosure; announcement via SETLink and SET News within 1 business day of Board resolution and in any case before the next SET trading session if price-sensitive.
Selective Disclosure/Blackout: No selective disclosure to analysts/investors; insiders will observe a trading blackout from circulation of Board materials until 24 hours after SET publication of the transaction notice and financial statements.
MNPI Scope Examples: Draft Board deck contains an unaudited Q2 revenue bridge, a proposed 3% list-price increase in July, and synergy targets for EPH not yet announced and therefore inside information relative to Siam Meridian securities.
Public/Non-MNPI References: As already announced in Siam Meridian’s press release dated 15 November 2024 and filed on SET, the Bangna plant automation pilot is complete; widely reported draft biomass tariff discussions in national media are public and not MNPI.
Negative/Non-issuer Update: The Port Authority of Eastern Gulf’s maintenance schedule for Berth C is an external operational update and, by itself, is not issuer-specific material information.
Financial Covenants/Undertakings: Maintain Net Debt/EBITDA ≤ 3.5x post-close; no dividend beyond 40% of NPAT until leverage ≤ 2.5x; proceeds applied only to EPH acquisition and transaction fees.
Timeline: Signing targeted 20 June 2026; Board meeting 18 June 2026; filing of the Information Memorandum and summary on SET on signing date; closing within 90 days post-CPs.
Other Terms: Break fee THB 30,000,000 if seller breaches exclusivity; non-binding except for confidentiality, exclusivity, and governing law; no change-in-control at Siam Meridian without debenture holder consent.
Contacts: Primary contact Khun Pramote Anuwat, CFO, pramote.anuwat@siammeridian.example; legal contact Ms. Chariya Vongpaisal, Head of Corporate Legal, chariya.v@siammeridian.example.
Confidentiality/Governing Law: This term sheet and any personal data will be handled under confidentiality and PDPA; Thai law governs; if disclosure is delayed under permitted SET practice, a trading halt may be requested to prevent disorderly markets.
